description_long: This is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a distinctive yellow and red color scheme and the branding 'aurigny.com' on its fuselage. The aircraft features a high-wing configuration with straight wings and twin engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with multiple windows, suggesting a multi-seat arrangement. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel under the cockpit. The overall design is streamlined for efficient flight, with clear demarcations for doors and windows.
description: A small, yellow and red high-wing airplane with twin wing-mounted engines, a conventional tail, and 'aurigny.com' branding. It features an enclosed forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the high-mounted straight wings, twin propeller engines, and the nose with cockpit windows.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the 'aurigny.com' branding, cockpit windows, and the full length of the fuselage and tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the back of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite side of the fuselage, cockpit, and tail, with visible engine and landing gear details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
